### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-02
> I spent about two hours trying to get the nvidia drivers installed since the scrolling is slower than molasses in winter, but ended up breaking the entire install....well not really I'm sure, but I didn't know how to fix it from the CLI and of course couldn't look up anything on how to without a browser.

use w3m as your browser if you have a broken xserver; just type in Ctrl-Alt-F1 login and type in: w3m

to fix your xserver, type in: sudo dpkg-reconfigure -fnoninteractive xserver-xorg

to install nvidia drivers, just install nvidia-glx from the repos: sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx

then type in: sudo nvidia-glx-config enable

> Guess I'll have to look elsewhere for how to install stuff (have any tips???)

these cover just about everything:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/6.10/ubuntu/desktopguide/C/index.html](https://help.ubuntu.com/6.10/ubuntu/desktopguide/C/index.html)
[http://ubuntuguide.org](http://ubuntuguide.org)
[http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntuinstall](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntuinstall)

### Post by ago on 2007-03-02
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> Did you try to run umount before rebooting? That fixes it for me

The new reboot script should do that, there should be no need for it. If it doesn't I'd like to know why. Try to mount the host partition via fstab or CLI, then reboot by running the commands in /etc/rc6.d/ one by one. Pay attention to S20* S40* S60*.

for x in /etc/rc6.d/K*; do sudo $x stop; done
sudo /etc/rc6.d/S15* stop
sudo /etc/rc6.d/S20* stop
....

### Post by Aliarse on 2007-03-02
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> check these folders:

/media/host
/media/windows
/ntfs

your windows drive should be there, if not:
```

sudo -s
mkdir /media/windows
NTFSPART=$(fdisk -l | grep NTFS | sed -e 's/\s/\n/g' | grep dev)
mount $NTFSPART /media/windows -t ntfs -o nls=utf8,umask=0222
exit
```

then it should be in /media/windows

are you using the experimental wubi-0.0.0.8 or old v3 version?

Well im back again. :)

Tried using this command, but it doesn't work, i still don't see my windows drive, and i REALLY need to see it so i can make my home.img bigger as i've only got 88mb left.

It used to work on the old 0.2 version when it was placed in the fstab file. I did try the latest version for herd 4, but it kept locking up after 10 mins of use so im back on the 0.3 version. Anything else i can try to get my current install to see my windows drive? :confused:  

Dont really want to have to reinstall everything all over again as i've got it setup exactly how i want it.

---

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-02
Aliarse, try this instead (make sure you have ntfs-3g installed before trying it):

```
sudo -s
mkdir /media/windows
NTFSPART=$(fdisk -l | grep NTFS | sed -e 's/\s/\n/g' | grep dev)
ntfs-3g $NTFSPART /media/windows
exit
```

then check /media/windows for your files, and when you're done:

```
sudo umount /media/windows
```

### Post by ago on 2007-03-04
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> Well I've updated the site to de-uglify it (replaced a couple of buttons, transitioning towards a frame-based model) see if you like the changes... [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup)

I am not an expert in web design by any stretch of the imagination, but as far as I know, frames should be avoided.

If we are using frames to simplify the maintanance of pages which are mostly similar, then there is little point, since I will write a script to generate the static pages from a template and some localized content (with no tags). This will also greatly simplify translations of the website (to be handled by rosetta). As for download times, the image is cached and the rest is maybe a few K so no big deal. 
    
By the way we should use http redirects via the webserver and not with "meta" tags.

> By the way, Ago, do we really want to have that Wubiworld.jpg background? It looks rather obtrusive... I think we should remove it or at least make it a bit smaller and less obtrusive... Also, it doesn't fit in with the standard ubuntu orange-brown color scheme.

The idea is to remind people of BOTH Ubuntu AND Windows. Ubuntu has the sun, Windows has the green hills (which spares us the trouble of using their logo). And it all fits together. Orange/Brown has no place in it, it violates the color scheme and it is alien to windows users. They have time to find out :D , the aim here us to make the windows users feel comfortable and a background that reminds of their windows one, does just that... 

Therefore no brown/orange. We should use white/blue buttons (picking the colors from wubiworld and possibly resembling a cloud). There should be an hoover effect since it improves usability. I have already stretched my web skills, I am sure others can pick up from here.
    
I agree that the image should be smaller, I will change that later on. It cannot just be resized since it has to fade smoothly into white.

> Also, if you want to test the site to see if it'll work under IE, use ies4linux 
Tux, I am aware of that but I do not want to waste hours trying to discover how MS screwed up the web standards. There are plenty of web devs which are familiar of IE intricacies. Hopefully one of them can jump on board, and (s)he will be able fix the issues in 5 mins while it will take me lots of annoying trials and errors.

---

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-04
> I am not an expert in web design by any stretch of the imagination, but as far as I know, frames should be avoided.

If we are using frames to simplify the maintanance of pages which are mostly similar, then there is little point, since I will write a script to generate the static pages from a template and some localized content (with no tags). This will also greatly simplify translations of the website (to be handled by rosetta). As for download times, the image is cached and the rest is maybe a few K so no big deal.

Why shouldn't we use frames? They improve compatibility with browsers such as IE. Your recent changes completely broke rendering on IE 5.5 and IE 6; the buttons also happen to be invisible due to their lack of alpha handling.

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-04
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> Why shouldn't we use frames? They improve compatibility with browsers such as IE. Your recent changes completely broke rendering on IE 5.5 and IE 6; the buttons also happen to be invisible due to their lack of alpha handling.

From what I can read frames have been deprecated (they are not even in the 1.1 XHTML standard, and our site will be XHTML compliant). As mentioned they serve no real purpose since we can use a proper templeting system to the same effect. Also no style attributes should be used in the html pages, since we already have a css file. If the site is broken it should be sufficient to fix the CSS directly. Finally, the least buttons we have with embedded text, the easiest it is to localize and maintain the website. 

[www.webheadstart.org/xhtml/xhtml11](www.webheadstart.org/xhtml/xhtml11) :
"Summary of Key Changes in XHTML 1.1. Support for HTML frames dropped."

So can you please revert to the previous website and try to fix that properly instead?

In the meantime I have moved my original site to: [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/wubi/ago/](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/wubi/ago/) so that relative links are not broken. It would be good practice to do the same for different versions to avoid overwriting each other, while making it possible to play with the website. Can we removed "en-US/old", or is it of any use?

---

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-05
IF nothing is working
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade
sudo apt-get dist-upgrade

IF the graphical is interface is not working

try from command line
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
OR if you have NVIDIA video card AND installed nvidia drivers
sudo nvidia-xconfig
OR if you have ATI video card AND have installed ATI drivers
sudo aticonfig –initial

### Post by ago on 2007-03-06
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> 've noticed that ago's preseed.cfg had a few errors and didn't actually fix the rebooting issuel

"calls sed, grep, awk, and xargs which aren't built into the initrd"

Awk, xargs & co are not executed by the initrd, but by the installed version of Ubuntu, so they will be there (under /usr/bin). The only command executed by the initrd is sed, which is available, and it is used to modify the rc6.d scripts. 

Since commands like  "/target/usr/bin/awk "\{print \\\$1\}" \|  /target/usr/bin/xargs -n1 kill" will be executed only inside Ubuntu, and since there is no /target/usr/bin in there, I think that your fix actually broke stuff... 

By the way, why exactly is the reboot not working? It works in my case. 

As for the website, if you wish to change the looks, please keep all the visual elements, including image sources, inside a separate css. This way it will be possible to change the look and feel by simply swapping the css and it will be easier for Joshier and the others to work on the website.

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-06
> **ago said:**
> By the way, why exactly is the reboot not working? It works in my case. 


In fact the problem with reboot is that the preseed.cfg bundled with wubi.exe is an old one, the issue is not that the scripts are wrong, but that there are no scripts at all...

### Post by ago on 2007-03-06
> **ecology2007 said:**
> I was thinking these days about making a 6.10 version to wait until then.
Even if it's not technically difficult to bundled up for Edgy, I would prefer people to test and report on Feisty, since the deadline is fairly close. Most issues would be in common, but there might be some specific nuisances we'd better know about in advance.

> My one cent is to release at feisty release + 1 week, to give time to official servers to absorb bandwith.
I think that wubi at the moment is only a fraction of the actual downloads, that will hopefully change soon ;)

We should try to be "in line",  since otherwise 

1) we will be answering calls of people that have downloaded a newer ISO which is not compatible with the current version of Wubi, 
2) people may end up using even more bandwidth, by downloading the old ISO and then downloading all the packages required for the upgrade. 
3) If there is heavy demand our server will probably go down well before the Ubuntu mirrors, even if it is "only" 10MB.

---

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-06
> Awk, xargs & co are not executed by the initrd, but by the installed version of Ubuntu, so they will be there (under /usr/bin). 

Whoops, forgot about that, I undid that part

> The only command executed by the initrd is sed, which is available, and it is used to modify the rc6.d scripts.

No it isn't; check the contents of your initrd; (gunzip, then cpio < iv initrd) bin/sed is NOT there; therefore, yes, the fix WAS necessary.

> Since commands like "/target/usr/bin/awk "\{print \\\$1\}" \| /target/usr/bin/xargs -n1 kill" will be executed only inside Ubuntu, and since there is no /target/usr/bin in there, I think that your fix actually broke stuff...

I've reverted that part, but I've kept the sed fix, becuase as I was saying, sed is NOT built into the initrd, so directly calling sed like it was in the old version won't do anything. It should be working fine now with my latest revision in bazaar.

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-06
> **godd4242 said:**
> Ok I got the installation to complete from a .ISO on my desktop.
Said installation complete etc etc etc.

I reboot, and no option for Ubuntu or anything.

Just tried XP boot options, only OS available was XP professional

Any ideas on what I've done wrong?

Always give details about your drives configuration when your asking for help (how many drives, how many windows, how many partitions, etc ...)

Check if you have at the root of your boot drive
boot.ini
menu.lst
grldr
grub


Find the boot ini file and check if you have a timeout value (0 is not a good value :) )
Add these lines if they are missing.
timeout=30
c:\grldr="Ubuntu"

DO NOT TOUCH THE OTHERS LINES RELATED TO WINDOWS.

Check the content of menu.lst if it's not that.

I'm just running straight up basic Windows Xp professional service pack 2 if that's any help.

Only 40 minutes to go on the .ISO download too...

EDIT:
Alright found the boot.ini.backup
But no just regular boot.ini
Which is disconcerting.
and mildly frightening.
But if you could explain what you mean by "root" I'd be happy to play Russian roulette with my computer :3

Yep boot.ini doesn't exist in C:\WINDOWS
Not fun...

By root i mean c:\ if you only have one drive.

Reboot and as soon, as the computer starts, hold the F8 key for 5 minutes (or at least until windows starts) 
Don't stop even if it keeps beeping.

If it does not help :

If you're afraid : the best solution is probably to reinstall wubi.
If you're not afraid :
Start Menu
Execute
cmd
attrib -r -h -s c:\boot.ini

Now look if you see c:\boot.ini in windows explorer.
You probably have a timeout set to 0, which gives you 0 seconds to select ubuntu at start.


When you're done
Start Menu
Execute
cmd
attrib +r +h +s c:\boot.ini

---

### Post by godd4242 on 2007-03-06
> **ecology2007 said:**
> By root i mean c:\ if you only have one drive.
If you're afraid : the best solution is probably to reinstall wubi.
If you're not afraid :
Start Menu
Execute
cmd
attrib -r -h -s c:\boot.ini

Now look if you see c:\boot.ini in windows explorer.
You probably have a timeout set to 0, which gives you 0 seconds to select ubuntu at start.


When you're done
Start Menu
Execute
cmd
attrib +r +h +s c:\boot.ini

Ok.
c:\boot.ini =
[boot loader]
timeout=30
default=multi(0)disk(0)rdisk(0)partition(1)\WINDOWS
[operating systems]
multi(0)disk(0)rdisk(0)partition(1)\WINDOWS="Microsoft Windows XP Professional" /noexecute=optin /fastdetect /forceresetreg

Timeout = 30 which is good of course.
My guess is I used an ISO not compatible with WUBI
Which is fine since the new patch is only 5 minutes away from done.
I'll reboot then and hold down f8 and hopefully have the option to start Ubuntu

That correct?

You're a god among men.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> I've created a script to automatically generate the Wubi NSIS installer directly from the wubi and lupin bazaar branches, it makes the creation of new releases much simpler, it's at [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh) use it to create new releases; just run **sh buildwub.sh** and supply the needed info.

Nice work, I like it! 

A few remarks. 
[list]
[*] Devs should take this as an opportunity to actually reduce the number of releases, since it is now easier for other devs/testers to fill the gaps in between. I think we should avoid the temptation to upload a new "release" for any line of code changed. It would be almost impossible to support users with so many releases, and it will create a lot of confusion. If people want to see daily builds we can automate the process and have proper "daily" builds uploaded every day in a daily-builds folder (suffixed by the date) while keeping the release and experimental folders clean, but I do not think it is necessary.

[*] Each uploaded release should come with a matching source file. See [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/wubi/downloads/experimental/wubi-7.04-herd4-v1-src.tgz](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/wubi/downloads/experimental/wubi-7.04-herd4-v1-src.tgz) . Tux, it would be a nice addon to give the option to also pack the sources (after cleaning their dirs of any binary, i.e. make clean && tar -czvf).

[*] We should create a "current" symlink or a redirect in the webserver pointing to the latest "release", so that we only have to change that. [/list]

As for wubi, great work there as well. My main suggestion here is to streamilne the Wubi interface quite a bit. There are far too many dialogs and popups, somehow it reminds people of those aggressive websites. In this regard I would: 

[list]
[*] Drop bittorrent, it is causing more troubles than it's worth and it increases the size of wubi quite a lot, let's keep the nsis hooks but remove the addon, it will be easy to add it back in the future if we need it. For now we should simply use http download with automatic mirror selection. 

[*] Remove the splash screen, music file and what not, less is more

[*] Remove all the confirmation dialogs, if there is any important message it should simply be embedded within the main wizard dialog. 

[*] Language selector: can we merge it with the Ubuntu installation locale selector and place it in the wizard dialog? 

[*] cdburner: I do not think we have the right to distriute it, it should be dropped until the situation is clear. 

[*] Most of the expert options can be moved into a separate wubi-tools application. A big plus if we can remove the normal/expert chooser alltogether while keeping the installation idiot-proof.[/list]

Edit: I have added the above points to a wubi spec: [https://blueprints.launchpad.net/wubi/+spec/simplify-wubi](https://blueprints.launchpad.net/wubi/+spec/simplify-wubi)

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> sed is NOT built into the initrd
Sed is not run by the lupin initrd directly, but by the Alternate ISO initrd, which includes sed.

### Post by Aquashark on 2007-03-07
i installed wubi in E:\wubi\ with the latest installer (v5) with Herd4, but the E: partition isn't mounted, only C: is.
i tried to mount it automatically through /etc/fstab, but it says "device sda5 busy" at startup. sudo mount returns busy also.

after that apparently everything worked.

That is a nice good report with lots of information.
You get to an advanced stage of the debian installation, that means all the wubi mechanism have been correctly executed and you are now in standard install mode.
Search in general forum is there is a known compatibility bewteen feisty and your hardware. 
If not :
-save your feisty iso file somewhere.
-delete wubi folder.
-make sure your had disk is clean and defragmented, by mouse left clicking on it in windows, and chosing properties.
-make sure you have more than 8 gb free.
-use wubi and select expert mode.
-manually use the iso file you saved on 1 step.
-leave the other settings untouched.

Come here again and yell at me because it did not solve anything.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **ddover said:**
> "C"s fill the screen and system freezes (I cry)
Just wait a little bit, normally it should resume and continue, it is processing stuff, I did not bother to fix the Cs since the plan is to change the installer interface.... If it lasts for a few minutes then there is an issue, in this case you should be able press Alt+F4 and have more info or Alt+F2 and then "nano /var/log/syslog"

> also after installing in Windows and rebooting: the first screen of the Ubuntu setup was some weird dialogue asking me to press some keys on my keyboard. only to find out in Ubuntu that i have the keyboard set to 106-keys Japanese (i use US English).
That is probably due to the locale being mispelled in preseed.cfg.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
By the way what is the reason of the gap in the version number? Is it because of local builds that increase the version number? In that case the version number should be inputed manually (simply renaming the files will do, since uploading files should not be a frequent event). Gaps in the version numbers on the public server should be avoided.

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-07
> **ago said:**
> 
That is probably due to the locale being mispelled in preseed.cfg.


I commented this in preseed cfg and i d like to have your point of view about it.
```

## KEYBOARD
# Keyboard selection.
[COLOR="Blue"]#d-i console-setup/modelcode string pc104
#d-i console-setup/layoutcode string us
[/COLOR]# To select a variant of the selected layout (if you leave this out, the
# basic form of the layout will be used):
#d-i console-setup/variantcode string dvorak

```
Added this instead
```

#d-i console-tools/archs select at
d-i console-keymaps-at/keymap select us

```

Because i can fetch in windows registry the parameter for the keymaps but not for the layoutcode. 
d-i console-keymaps-at/keymap select [COLOR="Blue"]us[/COLOR] is dynamically changed by wubi on install according to the windows setting.

I will come with a workaround soon if the 2 commands are not equivalent.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **ecology2007 said:**
> Noted ! Release with any version number you like.
My numbering source side was :
V2 unreleased : password input bug fix
V3 unrealeased :http download for expert mode
V4 unrealeased :http dowload for both modes, preseed reboot fix.
V4 released by tux :1st autobuild same as V4.
V5 released by tux :Timezones, locale and keyboard detection.

I advise you to use this V4 as stable, since it as no issue on wubi side.
[http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/wubi/downloads/experimental/wubi-7.04-herd4-v4.exe](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/wubi/downloads/experimental/wubi-7.04-herd4-v4.exe)

If a package is not released, the version number should not change. You should use a different numbering scheme for local builds, using timestamp for instance, then once you decide something can be uploaded, you reneme it with the appropriate version number. I would also suggest that before we publish something we "pass" it around to other members of the lupin-team, give them a chance to test the current devel branches, and comment on it, then send it up. That will slow things down a bit, but hopefully it will be for the better. As for the current versions we might have to remove them (including v1), since I think that we are violating the license of the CDBurner bundled and it looks to me that its license is incopmpatible with GPL2 (I am no lawyer but when in doubt I prefer to be on the safe side). So we might want to "start" again from herd5. In short, do not bother to rename the files.

I would also suggest that before we publish something we "pass" it around to other members of the lupin-team, give them a chance to test the current devel branches, and comment on it, then send it up. That will slow things down a bit, but hopefully it will be for the better. As for the current versions we might have to remove them (including v1), since I think that we are violating the license of the CDBurner bundled and it looks to me that its license is incopmpatible with GPL2 (I am no lawyer but when in doubt I prefer to be on the safe side). So we might want to "start" again from herd5. In short, do not bother to rename the files.

I will follow this behavior from now on :)
If no one is working on setup-ubuntu.nsi from the current wubi repository now, i'm gonna work on it a few hours and add some conditional compilation flags so most of your remarks will be taken into account (torrents, popups, splash screen, cd burner).

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-07
To ago :
What do you mean when you say in [https://blueprints.launchpad.net/wubi/+spec/simplify-wubi](https://blueprints.launchpad.net/wubi/+spec/simplify-wubi)
> * Language selector: can we merge it with the Ubuntu installation locale selector and place it in the wizard dialog?
I don't know what is he the Ubuntu installation locale selector.
What i do know :
-When you boot the alternate CD, you press F2, choose your language and it sets up the keyboard automagically.
-The current Wubi allready does not need any input of the user to select the languages correctly for lupin.
-The NSIS code has not been designed to use translations. I don't have the knowledge to do it without a major rework.

As a rule when you post a bug report always tell us as much as you can (eg number of disks, numbers of windows, if your computer is more than 10 years old or has 4 external usb drives and such stuff ...)
Then, if you have a standard computer,
- Just make room on your hard disk ( at least 10 GB free) 
- Go to "My computer", select your disk, left mouse clic, properties, check disk, then defrag. 

Go to c:\wubi.
Find the feisty herd 4 iso.
Copy feisty herd 4 iso somewhere else and remember where.
Delete c:\wubi
Press Empty Trash
Restart the Wubi installer.
Expert.
Loopback Install.
Select Use ISO (You remember where it is, right ?).
Press Next
Press Next
Press Next
Sit and wait
When asked for reboot press yes.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
I have added the wubi webiste as a bazar branch. This should simplify the maintenance of the website, help with translations (via rosetta) and avoid problems with simultaneous uploads (it already happened to me and tux!). The directories are structured so that later on we can use a script to generate the static pages to simplify localization (possibly based on the mako templating engine). The actual html pages are inside the public folder. All other folders are empty at the moment.

[https://launchpad.net/~lupin-team/+branch/wubi/website](https://launchpad.net/~lupin-team/+branch/wubi/website)

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **ecology2007 said:**
> I commented this in preseed cfg and i d like to have your point of view about it.
```

## KEYBOARD
# Keyboard selection.
[COLOR="Blue"]#d-i console-setup/modelcode string pc104
#d-i console-setup/layoutcode string us
[/COLOR]# To select a variant of the selected layout (if you leave this out, the
# basic form of the layout will be used):
#d-i console-setup/variantcode string dvorak

```
Added this instead
```

#d-i console-tools/archs select at
d-i console-keymaps-at/keymap select us

```

With the above, I am asked to identify the keyboard by d-i, therefore I reverted to the previous settings. Anyway, the idea of preseed.cfg is that it is only a template. Wubi should do string substitution to set the appropriate parameters according to the info collected. I have added a few bug fixes. There are still a few error messages at the beginning but they should not be relevant. All in bazaar.

EDIT: hmmm not sure why but I always override other bzr revisions, I apologize for that, I will use a good-old checkout style from now on.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **tribaal said:**
> Oh well? Should I then install this using wine?
Are you joking? :D 
It runs perfectly under Linux. 
In practice you have to get the Lupin sources, run make to build the initrd, copy the dist folder to / and append the menu.lst entries provided. We wrote a few scripts to automate the process (the precursors to lubi) but the scripts need to be updated.

[http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=359614](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=359614)

For now we only have a regular http download for the installer we will probably add bittorrent ones later on. Thanks for that.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **mwilley said:**
> 
I get some other stuff (not much, but I was just about done with writing) and then get thrown to the BusyBox command thing.
Most of the above are known non fatal bugs which should mostly have been fixed in the new code, other than the error messages they should have no effect though. As for busybox, if you started in debug mode it is normal, otherwise it is not.

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-07

Sure, done. New script at [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh)
 
> I have added the wubi webiste as a bazar branch. This should simplify the maintenance of the website, help with translations (via rosetta) and avoid problems with simultaneous uploads (it already happened to me and tux!). The directories are structured so that later on we can use a script to generate the static pages to simplify localization (possibly based on the mako templating engine). The actual html pages are inside the public folder. All other folders are empty at the moment.

Sounds fine, I've just added a redirector and modified your branch to not use direct download links, so that people uploading new releases will only have to change the [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest.html) and [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest-src.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest-src.html) files instead of the bazaar branch and the website index; maybe I can add this to the autobuilder script as well, to have it automatically upload the newly generated releases...

Anyhow, I've just autobuilt a new release based on the latest changes in wubi, see [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest.html) for the installer and [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest-src.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/latest-src.html) for source.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-07
> **Aliarse said:**
> 
Please, anybody, (im literally begging you)! Help me get my windows partition working. If you need any info, tell me what you need and i'll get you it asap as i need this fixing so i can resize my home.img.

For the windows partion it should be sufficient to run 

sudo apt-get install ntfs-3g  (assuming you have internet working_
sudo ntfs-3g /dev/hdXY /mnt (replace XY with appropriate values /dev/hda1 for disk1 partition1)

For expanding a drive, see [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=2194195&postcount=739](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=2194195&postcount=739)

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-08
2 improvements to the installer autobuilder at [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh)

it can now automatically upload the autogenerated installer and sources to the cutlersoftware site and link the latest version to [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental.html) and [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental.html)

it can now accept input for the version number and adjust the nsis script accordingly, so you no longer have to modify the wubi nsis script to change the version

I was asked to reboot-did so -chose ubuntu in the BIOS and then got scrolling pages of words and numbers. These eventually stopped on a page where all the lines began 47. The final line had "error_code+0x7c/0x90" at the end of it. After that was a 47 line with two rows of dots and below that a flashing horizontal cursor. Nothing happens after that so I have to escape via power button.
I have tried this about six times now and noticed that it is not always the same page that things seize up- sometimes it's 51 or 48, although the error code above seems to feature somewhere.
I also tried the other choices in the initial stages-ubuntu debug and something about dynamic install. The debug option behaved pretty much the same as above only different pages showed until it stalled on one and no further. The dynamic choice stalled fairly swiftly and seemed to be prompting me to type in help. I did so and a section of commands (?) appeared but I have no idea what they mean or what to do with them.
My system is about 18 months old- a Celeron r 3,06ghz, 80gb c drive, 120gb d drive and one gig of RAM. I have at least 4 usb ports and an nVidia graphics card and am running XP home. There is 49gb free space on the c drive.

Please help I'm going mad with this. Thanks in advance. johnwillyums
If the install has crashed once, you need to reset the files that are used for linux virtual hard disks to blank new ones.
Because if anything is on it, it will boot as if they were correctly installed, and not boot in install mode.
The easiest way to reset them is to delete menu.lst, wubi folders on all you drives, and reinstall again using the one of the iso you have on your folder. But if one of this iso is corrupted, the install will crash again.

---

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-08
> **Brandonson112 said:**
> Ok.. I finally found out what my problem is  my computer cannot find menu.lst in any directory. Any help?
Delete all menu.lst files you find on the root of your hard drives, delete wubi folder on all your hard drives. If you have 2 wubi folders it will crash at boot.
Reinstall.

> I do not think the latest lupin sources have been bundled yet. They should be tested by the other devs first.

Haven't tested it, but I autobuilt/autouploaded it using my nifty script at [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/buildwubi.sh)

installer at [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental.html)
source at [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental-src.html](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/experimental-src.html)

If it's working, just run this script and it'll make the experimental version the latest release, and update all the links so that they point to the latest version: [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/newversion.sh](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/newversion.sh)

I've also added in a small link to the experimental version on the main site, under the main download button, for the people who want bleeding-edge code, and to help in the testing process of experimental builds.

Also, would you mind periodically running the script after making major changes? It only takes around 4 minutes for the script to automatically build, bundle, and upload the packages to the experimental version on DSL, even less if you're on a fast connection, and it would save me the trouble of keeping track of the revisions in the branches and rebooting to feisty just to build the packages...

### Post by ago on 2007-03-09
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> 
Also, would you mind periodically running the script after making major changes? It only takes around 4 minutes for the script to automatically build, bundle, and upload the packages to the experimental version on DSL, even less if you're on a fast connection, and it would save me thegtrouble of keeping track of the revisions in the branches and rebooting to feisty just to build the packages...

Tux, I have already tried your script and I think it is really cool. But, as mentioned before, I would like to avoid uploading too many version for 2 reasons essentially: 

1) When a user asks for support, I won't be able to do much if I cannot remember the changes of each of the different versions floating around (it is already quite challenging), instead of helping them out we will end up repeating the mantra "use the latest version", and by the time they do so another one may popup... That will make bug discovery harder and it will be annoying for users. 

2) By the time we reach final there should be a single version and we should not touch it. So it is better to get used to the idea of having few releases gradually. The final is only 1 month and a half ahead.


In this regard I think that we should make the process of uploading a release far stricter. A person should be in charge of it and upload only when there are significant changes to the code and everything is stable enough. Since you have been doing that since the beginning why don't you take care of that? 

I would therefore create a version of the script that can upload, which you keep for yourself, and one that is used for testing purposes and does not upload anything. (PS in the script can you put everything inside a new folder in the current directory as opposed to ~/.* ?).

Also I do not think that untested versions should be uploaded at all, we can test among ourselves and more advanced users via your script, then when we have all green flags, we can publish. So I am not sure the experimental link should be there.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-09
You start in debug mode, then when you are at the prompt you scroll with shift+pgup and see if there is any relevant error message, errors about grep and tty you can disregard, you should look for errors about not finding the right ISO or being unable to find the host folder. Look if you have a host folder (ls /host/).

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-09
> **ago said:**
> Thanks for that, the plan is to create a script to generate the webpages from simple text files. This will make translations easier since you will not have to handle any html. I did not have time yet to work on that. As soon as I do so, I will activate rosetta in launchpad and you are more than welcome to help us out.

One of the most common reasons you get a prompt is if the installation was interrupted previously and then you try again. What happens is that the disk images are no longer vergin, and if the disk images are not vergin, the installer will try to boot as opposed to install, but if the installation did not complete previously, it will obviously fail and you get the shell. In short if the installation is interrupted for any reason AFTER you get to the page with the progress bars, you have to delete and recreate root.img.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-09
> **johnwillyums said:**
> 
slumber= and a rapid countdown from around 2000 I think.
That's the main init process waiting for the root device. There should be some message about trying to mount a device berfore the slumber messages start. Init should try to mount /dev/ram0 and it is strange that it does not find it.

> There was no sign of (is/host/)
That shuold be the LS command in lower case and with a space after it and a folder name. If you run `ls /` you will see what folder are there and `ls /host/` for a list of files inside /host

### Post by Frak on 2007-03-09
> **ago said:**
> I must have been leaving under a stone but what exactly is a Metadata downloader?
By the way the bittorrent downloader should be dropped in the next versions in favor of an http downloader with automatic mirror selection.
A Metadata downloader is a mix of HTTP/FTP/P2P downloader, it opens multiple streams to the download location and downloads this way, but it *also* downloads via P2P (Torrent), thus not only saving bandwidth, but making a download run at the speed of light, and the download can be paused for any reason at all without data loss.

More info [here.]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metalink")

> "Metalink is an open standard/framework for programs that download (download managers, BitTorrent clients, Web browsers, FTP clients, & P2P programs). For increased convenience, it stores the many locations of files (FTP/HTTP/P2P) in a single file (a .metalink) for extra reliability in case one method fails and so chunks/segments of each file can be downloaded from multiple resources at the same time (known as accelerated/multi-threaded/segmented downloading)."

EDIT
Sorry in my last post, I meant 3**MB/s**, not 3**GB/s**.

More info [here.]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metalink")

### Post by ago on 2007-03-09
> **johnwillyums said:**
> . Thanks for your help and patience any suggestions where to go from here. johnwillyums

Edit menu.lst and replace "debug=2" with "debug". At the prompt run "more /tmp/*" (watch the space!). Now you should be able to scroll. 

It might be difficult for you to spot the right number. You may want to run "cp /tmp/* /host/wubi/installation.log" (there are 2 spaces in there, do not miss them). If the cp command works, the installation.log will be in your wubi folder and you can attach it here.

[aria2]("http://aria2.sourceforge.net") does have a Windows port of version 0.9.0 but it's command line. Also, you really want the latest version for the features. Maybe if enough people on Windows are interested, they can figure out the problems with porting the latest version.

I'm really interessted in the commandline version.
If you re a little bit power user, would you be kind enough to make some tests with it, find the better options, resume procedures, test it on feisty isos ? We are specially interested in resume and integrity checks more than speed, as official mirrors provide us decent http speed.

Thanks forward.

---

### Post by antini on 2007-03-09
> **ecology2007 said:**
> I'm really interessted in the commandline version.
If you re a little bit power user, would you be kind enough to make some tests with it, find the better options, resume procedures, test it on feisty isos ? We are specially interested in resume and integrity checks more than speed, as official mirrors provide us decent http speed.

Thanks forward.

Definitely. I'm not a power user but I'm really familiar with aria2.

aria2 automatically resumes but you need to use an option to do so.

0.9.0 only offers a full file checksum at the end of transfer. This is handy for users who aren't familiar with the checksum verification process. But, if there's an error in transfer, the full file checksum won't match and you have to start over from the beginning. Not good. Pluses: Compiles on Windows now, also the version that's in feisty repos (yum install aria2).

0.10.1+ features integrity checks/automatic error correction during the download. It uses chunk checksums like BT, so all downloads with chunk checksums are guaranteed error free (barring any bugs that the program might have - haven't seen any yet affecting this tho) and the user doesn't do anything different. No trackers needed and none of the usual problems of P2P. Drawback: 1) Each file needs to be scanned and have a metalink generated (easy with automated tools). 2) This version doesn't compile on Windows at the moment (but 0.9.0 does).

I've also added in a small link to the experimental version on the main site, under the main download button, for the people who want bleeding-edge code, and to help in the testing process of experimental builds.

It only takes around 4 minutes for the script to automatically build, bundle, and upload the packages to the experimental version on DSL, even less if you're on a fast connection, and it would save me the trouble of keeping track of the revisions in the branches and rebooting to feisty just to build the packages...

As ago said, only requirement is to report progress in one way or another. And not to be too heavy.

As for NSIS visual integration of external software, it s too much trouble to be worth it.

### Post by Frak on 2007-03-09
Just downloaded an openSUSE DVD, it works fine, you just have to give it the correct syntax.
and I can create a metalink, not hard, its just an XML file

P.S. Unizip the folder, the precompiled one found [here]("http://smithii.com/files/aria2-0.9.0-win32-mingw.zip") and lead the command prompt to the directory and run ```
aria2c --DIR=(output directory) --OUT=(output name) -M (location of Metalink)
```

And I've used it before on many of my computers, and it hasn't crashed once.

### Post by antini on 2007-03-09
> **ecology2007 said:**
> 2 questions :
Are you sure the error correction applies too when it is downloading from http mirrors ?
Does it have known issues with firewalls and proxies like bittorent has ? We need zero config from the user.


If you or Frak have some experience with cygwin and bring me an exe compiled without the debug flags, i will integrate it in wubi. For now adding 3 megabytes is out of question.

At that time we will also have to get a metalink ready for feisty alternate iso.

Yes, error correction is done from http/ftp mirrors. from the aria2 page:

> aria2 supports chunk checksum in Metalink: it validates chunk checksums while downloading a file like BitTorrent. This gives safer and more reliable download experience.

This is for normal ftp/http downloads. There are options for proxies, so it's not zero config:

>  --http-proxy=HOST:PORT       Use HTTP proxy server. This affects to all
                              URLs.
 --http-user=USER             Set HTTP user. This affects to all URLs.
 --http-passwd=PASSWD         Set HTTP password. This affects to all URLs.
 --http-proxy-user=USER       Set HTTP proxy user. This affects to all URLs
 --http-proxy-passwd=PASSWD   Set HTTP proxy password. This affects to all URLs.

 --http-proxy-method=METHOD   Set the method to use in proxy request.
                              METHOD is either 'get' or 'tunnel'.
                              Default: tunnel
I'm not a developer, so I won't be providing an exe. There is a .metalink for herd 5 at [http://www.metalinker.org/samples/feisty-herd5-alternate-i386.iso.metalink](http://www.metalinker.org/samples/feisty-herd5-alternate-i386.iso.metalink)

It's plain XML so you can check it out and see the repair info/chunk checksums. While in feisty, you can test it out w/ 'aria2c URL' after you've installed aria2.

### Post by antini on 2007-03-09
> **Frak said:**
> Just downloaded an openSUSE DVD, it works fine, you just have to give it the correct syntax.
and I can create a metalink, not hard, its just an XML file

And I've used it before on many of my computers, and it hasn't crashed once.

Just thought I would echo this. There are cross platform tools for metalink creation. [metalink tools]("http://metalinks.sourceforge.net/") are command line. [Metalink Editor]("http://hampus.vox.nu/metalink/") has a GUI. Ubuntu, Kubuntu, and Edubuntu release ISOs are always at [http://download.packages.ro/metalink/](http://download.packages.ro/metalink/)

All software has bugs, but aria2 hasn't crashed on me. And if it does, the repair info (if it's in the .metalink) can be used to verify that there were no errors and resume error free. This is why openSUSE and other Linux/BSD distributions officially use metalink.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-09
> **Frak said:**
> Just got done with my metalink.
Can be found, when I get the URL.
The main requirements are:
1) the library should by thin, 2) it should be stable, 3) it should be easy to bundle with NSIS 4) it should work with zero config (or alternatively it should be possible to autodetect all settings). 

If all of the above can be satisfied, it's a green flag as far as I am concerned, otherwise we should probably postpone it to the next release.

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-10
> **Aliarse said:**
> bump... :-\" 8-[

I really don't know why you don't have a home.img, but  i you wanna give a try.

1 - if you have plenty of space back up your img files somewhere else.

2 - Search this thread for qemu-img.
In windows command shell, make a big extra.img with it.


3 - Reboot in ubuntu.
Search this thread for rsync.
Use it to copy your /root on extra.img
Delete the home folder on the extra.img

4- Reboot in windows
Rename it to newroot.img
Make a big new extra.img.

5 -Reboot in ubuntu.
Copy your home to extra.img using rsync.

6 -Reboot in windows.
Rename your root.img to old.root
Rename newroot.img to root.img, rename extra.img to home.img

7 -See that now nothing works anymore and reinstall.

Have fun.

Edit : Wait don't do that, i have just seen that you have a ubuntu.img instead of the root.img, i have no clue about what version you're using.

### Post by antini on 2007-03-10
> **ago said:**
> As mentioned, the c/c++ library should be used directly, I am sure that if there is a wx gui you can get the library separately. It is quite important to keep the size down, that is one of the main reasons for using nsis. I fear that including python + a widget set (wx or gtk) + cygwin would be too heavy.

The main requirements are:
1) the library should by thin, 2) it should be stable, 3) it should be easy to bundle with NSIS 4) it should work with zero config (or alternatively it should be possible to autodetect all settings).

If all of the above can be satisfied, it's a green flag as far as I am concerned, otherwise we should probably postpone it to the next release.


Hampus Wessman, who makes the GUI [Metalink Editor]("http://hampus.vox.nu/metalink/") in python, is taking this on. You already use python for the bt download, so he's doing to do it in that. No need for a widget set or cygwin.

It won't support segmented downloads from multiple mirrors, but it will support error autocorrection (chunk checksums in the metalink) and resume. And send progress info to the installer.

He's checking out your code, but can he just make a standard plugin?

### Post by antini on 2007-03-10
> **ecology2007 said:**
> Since you have found a way to make some metalinks, would you be nice enough you run some test and get sure that the files are really checked after download ?
Ie pause a download, break the file with any idea you can think of  and resume it ? 
An easy way to do that would be with 2 differrents files of the same size, or you can use any hex editor you want.
This with various metalinks containing torrents or not, sha1, md5, etc...
If you get good results you will probably get my green light too.:)

I didn't test it with torrents, but I did edit the chunk checksums for a file to be incorrect. I logged the transfer and aria2 expects those to be right, so it just keeps trying to reget that chunk. If the full file checksum is altered (but there are no chunk checksums) it just tells you the checksum failed. You wouldn't be using it without the chunk checksums tho...

### Post by ago on 2007-03-10
> **Mohammed Abbas said:**
> Hi there ,
Can I use the ubuntu windows installer to deploy ubuntu on pre formatted partition with ext3 (with swap) rather creating one file on NTFS partition ?
The thing is I don't have CD writer and this if possible would be great for people like me ..
Regards

Wubi can be installed inside ext3 but always inside a file. If you want actually to install inside a partition on several machines in the network you really may want to consider netboot install. 

[http://www.howtoforge.com/ubuntu_pxe_install_server](http://www.howtoforge.com/ubuntu_pxe_install_server)

For a single machine see:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=28948](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=28948)

---

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-10
> **antini said:**
> Can someone offer advice? Hampus is having some problems:

"I use the modified NSISdl that they use in wubi (from BattleTorrent). When I write "nsis.setupprogress()" the progress page is shown, but after my script has finished (when this page is supposed to dissappear) the program crashes.

if I try to update the progress it crashes immediately."

Battle Torrent uses an old NSISdl.dll version. NSIS now natively includes NSISdl.dll.
We did not want to erase it.
Look in the plugin folder of the source. There is a readme in it.
In our current version, with the battle torrent of our sources, when we call battletorrent, we use the nsisdl2.dll (which is the old one from battletorrent).

To compile, put an initrd.gz, linux and preseed.cfg in the ubuntu folder of the source.
(you can put dummy files if you don't have them)
To reactivate battle torrent in wubi at compilation, set the "torrent" flag to 1 on the top of the sources.

I sent a mail to him a few hours ago, tell him to check his mailbox.

See you.

I was planning to make a downloading plugin (metalinks, chunk checksum checking, resume) in python, similar to how BattleTorrent works. After experimenting a bit and reading what you have said here on the forum I think it would be better to make a plugin in C instead, based on the standard NSISdl. That way it would be smaller, faster and simpler (to use at least). I wouldn't need to rely on some old python plugins for my code to work either!

By modifying the NSISdl plugin I could make everything look the same, both for the user and for developers used to NSISdl. So you wouldn't need to change that much in your code! (you would probably want to use metalinks though)

This will take some time to finish, so you need to have some patience... What do you all think about the idea?

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-11
> I've got myself an account on the forum now! I'm the Hampus that antini told you about...

I was planning to make a downloading plugin (metalinks, chunk checksum checking, resume) in python, similar to how BattleTorrent works. After experimenting a bit and reading what you have said here on the forum I think it would be better to make a plugin in C instead, based on the standard NSISdl. That way it would be smaller, faster and simpler (to use at least). I wouldn't need to rely on some old python plugins for my code to work either!

By modifying the NSISdl plugin I could make everything look the same, both for the user and for developers used to NSISdl. So you wouldn't need to change that much in your code! (you would probably want to use metalinks though)

This will take some time to finish, so you need to have some patience... What do you all think about the idea?

Sounds great, we'll add it to the wubi tree once you've got the metalink downloader working; given that you're just using NSISdl, it should be able to be a drop-in replacement for the current http download method.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-11
> **Aliarse said:**
> 
Im not using the latest version, im using setup-ubuntu-full.exe Verson 3, which has no C:\wubi folder, only C:\ubuntu, and no home.img, just an ubuntu.img.

You need to upgrade to the latest version, to do so without reinstalling you need to:

1) do a full system update from ubuntu and install kernel 2.6.20-8-generic (use synaptic for that)
2) run the new installer and create the c:\wubi folder (do not reboot)
3) replace root.img inside wubi with the old one.

Now, when you reboot it should be almost like using a new system. If you have reboot problems there is a solution in the forum.

I used the wubi-7.04-herd4-v1 installer to install wubi on my computer. The install worked fine, and when I rebooted into Ubuntu the installation from the alternate cd iso worked. When I start the computer now and boot into Ubuntu, Ubuntu loads and I can log in, but my mouse will not move. This means that I can not do anything at all. Is there any way to fix this?
That's more likely a general Ubuntu bug, the version installed is also experimental. A full system update might fix the issue, look above how to upgrade the kernel without reinstalling everything.
 
> Also, is there any way to use this wubi installer to boot a live cd and then install from it onto the hard drive (.img files)?
Not in this version, it can easily be added but we do not fancy to give users too many options. It would complicate life for them and for us. You can do so manually in a few steps using hd-media installation (but it will require you to have a dedicated partion for ubuntu).

### Post by ago on 2007-03-11
> **imho74 said:**
> Hi,

I have an old 486 PC with Win95 running. Is it possible to install Ubuntu as server or as minimal installation on this machine with the "installer.exe"?

Yes you can, you have to edit preseed.cfg before rebooting and commenting out the desktop package at the end of the file replacing it with ubuntu-standard.

#tasksel	tasksel/first multiselect ubuntu-desktop
tasksel tasksel/first multiselect ubuntu-standard

### Post by HampusW on 2007-03-11
> **ecology2007 said:**
> This is a great idea, and we are in no way in a hurry, i am very curious about what you will use for the engine itself. Do you intend to use aria2 core with a NSIS wrapper ?

Right now I'm planning to use libcurl for the actual downloading. It has alot of features, but is also small and fast. You can find more details about libcurl on their website: [http://curl.haxx.se/libcurl/]("http://curl.haxx.se/libcurl/"). libcurl supports both http and ftp downloads in a fast and simple way. It doesn't support metalinks or checksums on its own though, so my plugin will have to provide that functionality. I will use some kind of hash lib for the checksums and a xml library for the metalink parsing. Haven't decided about those yet. All in all I'm quite sure the plugin will take less than 1 MB and probable more like half a MB.

So far I've downloaded libcurl and compiled a static library out of it and I've made a test app with some basic libcurl functionality. Works excellent! I've also tested to compile the NSISdl plugin and that works fine too, so I know everything I need is working (except xml & hashes, which should be no problem). Now I just need to put it all together and fill in the missing details... It's just a matter of time until it's all done and working! I'll keep you updated on my progress.

### Post by dbgeezer on 2007-03-11
> **tuxcantfly said:**
> I've autobuilt a new version, usual download spot ...

Tux,

I'm having the same problem on the first line of menu.lst; find always results in Error 17.

v4 doesn't do this, i don't know about v5, v6-v8 all give me this result.

BTW your find has two different syntaxes.  

Ubuntu = find /wubi/linux --set-root
Ubuntu debug = find --set-root /wubi/linux

Hope this helps:???:

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-11
> **dbgeezer said:**
> 
BTW your find has two different syntaxes.  

Ubuntu = find /wubi/linux --set-root
Ubuntu debug = find --set-root /wubi/linux

Hope this helps:???:

Hmmm it should be the second one. Moreover in the new version there should only be one menu option, not 2, and it should be debug. The grub menu can now also be hidden (uncomment "hiddenmenu"). The only menu.lst option should now read:

```
title Ubuntu
find --set-root /wubi/linux
kernel /wubi/linux find=/wubi/linux fb=false debug BOOT_DEBUG=2 priority=critical ro
initrd /wubi/initrd.gz
boot
```

If anything goes wrong AFTER grub, you should now find the logs under c:\wubi\logs, you can zip the folder and append it in a post. If anything goes wrong during the grub stage there are no logs. The best option in this case is to use the grub shell to try and see why the find command fails.

---

*****

### Post by ago on 2007-03-12
> **windozer said:**
> [Here is a link to the post.]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=2279067&postcount=1244") 
I'm not sure about what version I'm using.. I started with herd 4 and am almost completely updated (19 updates left). I can't install more updates yet because updating and installing stuff can take hours during which time the computer is unusable, due to being frozen most of the time.

Use "top" in a console to see which process is taking all the resources. Then run the responsible command in verbose mode from the console (generally using the -v flag). You can check disk space with the "df" command. For resizing see [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=2194195](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=2194195)

Old versions of wubi had a problem with sata drives, if your hard disk is sata that might be a reason.

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-12
> **LurkerLinux said:**
> I got exactly the same thing as you. 

But, on the bright side, v8 fixed the problems I was have with not being able to mount /mnt/sda1 on boot up. So that's one fixed. I did not attempt to alter the preseed in any way.

For a quick fix, you can place an old working pressed.cfg inside c:\wubi\install\ You can use the one inside the lupin project (in the src file).
Also use the following in menu.lst, replacing the othe entries.

> 
title Ubuntu
find --set-root /wubi/linux
kernel /wubi/linux find=/wubi/linux fb=false debug BOOT_DEBUG=2 priority=critical ro
initrd /wubi/initrd.gz
boot

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-12
> **windozer said:**
> Well I know its ntfs-3g causing the problem. Its like its just going into an infinite loop for some reason.
Is this an ntfs-3g process you launch yourself (to access the windows folders) or is it the one which is started at boot? In the second case the -v flag is useless, since the nfts-3g is started by the lupin initrd. 

One issue in the past, was that the disk images did not preallocate space, so any write operation was much slower.

---

### Post by windozer on 2007-03-12
> **ago said:**
> Is this an ntfs-3g process you launch yourself (to access the windows folders) or is it the one which is started at boot? In the second case the -v flag is useless, since the nfts-3g is started by the lupin initrd. 

One issue in the past, was that the disk images did not preallocate space, so any write operation was much slower.
I didn't know you could access the Windows folders! That would be a big time saver as I currently boot into Windows and copy stuff onto a USB key to use in ubuntu.. So it's the one thats started at boot.
I don't think my disk space was preallocated. I think in windows it shows the file as being 4 gigs, but with "size on disk" being much lower. Any way to fix this without reinstalling ubuntu?
But it's not writing to the drive at the time anyway, otherwise the hard drive would be making noise and the hard drive activity light would be on, right? There seems to be no hard drive activity at all during a freeze, just constant cpu usage. Ram usage keeps changing slightly, by a couple of megs up and down.

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-12
> **windozer said:**
> Any way to fix this without reinstalling ubuntu?
Not sure if it works but try to move the images to a different folder (or better to a different partition) and then back, that should force the OS to allocate the space. Make sure the size of the image file shows correctly under windows. It helps if the image is not fragmented. Also upgrade the kernel to 6.20-8-generic (use synaptic to do that), then extract and copy the initrd and kernel from the latest version of wubi into your wubi folder.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-12
It means that the source code has been tested with Herd5. But the bundled version has been built with herd4 and must be used with herd4. Next version will probably be a herd5 one, so you can start downloading the alternate ISO but you cannot use it with wubi yet. Ecology is working to fix some of the issues with V8, mentioned above, once that is done we'll test it first among the devs, then if it's ok, we will upload it.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-12
I have just seen a bug report about a new release I knew nothing about ([https://answers.launchpad.net/lupin/+ticket/4162](https://answers.launchpad.net/lupin/+ticket/4162) ) !!! This simply is not acceptable. Yet again, a release, has been uploaded untested by the other devs. This procedure is highly damaging for our project, and it creates inconveniences for the users. It should stop immediately. 

From now on, any new build will go into [http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/devel/minefield](http://cutlersoftware.com/ubuntusetup/devel/minefield) folder and it will stay there until the other lupin-team members give green light. In NO case a new build will be moved directly to the public download folder. There will be no link in the website to the minefield folder. Buildwubi.sh will be modified so that it can, at most, upload to minefield. 

All herd4 releases will be moved to the old relics section. We start from scratch with herd5, there should be no more than 1 or 2 version in Herd5 and v2 should only be used to fix major bugs.

### Post by dbgeezer on 2007-03-12
> **oscartheduck said:**
> Hi folks,

I just attempted an install with Wubi v9. Everything seems to have gone smoothly up to the reboot point.

After reboot I can select ubuntu from the menu that lists ubuntu and windows. I have two options, ubuntu or ubuntu =wubi setup version or some such thing.

Both of these options give me the same error. It's a grub error 17, /wubi/linux cannot be found. /wubi/linux exists as C:\wubi\linux. Should I leave a message here only, or report on launchpad, or wait until you folks update to herd5 and see if that resolves things?

1. Try downloading and installing v4.  
2. Grab C:\grldr and C:\wubi\install\preseed.cfg and copy them someplace else.
3. Uninstall v4.
4. Install v9.
5. Before you reboot, copy grldr and preseed.cfg back to their respective locations.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-12
> **zxg said:**
> I want to make a new initrd.gz to support feisty-desktop-i386.iso LIVECD.

And I found "sidux" livecd use [COLOR="Red"]squashfs[/COLOR] to boot ISO from harddisk,
sidux based on kanotix

[http://distrowatch.com/table.php?distribution=sidux](http://distrowatch.com/table.php?distribution=sidux)
download:  [http://debian.tu-bs.de/project/sidux/release/](http://debian.tu-bs.de/project/sidux/release/)

You can already boot a LiveCD ISO using lupin, but installing is a different matter. Wubi is based on alternate ISO, which use d-i, which require .deb packages. The Live ISO does NOT contain any packages only files which must be copied (see ubiquity). Therefore the installation mechanism, even if still based on d-i, is quite different. You can use lupin to modify a Live ISO as well. [https://launchpad.net/lupin](https://launchpad.net/lupin) feel free to ask.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-13
zxg,

If your aim is simply to boot the live ISO without burning the cd then you should look at instlux: [http://instlux.sourceforge.net/](http://instlux.sourceforge.net/) 
Wubi takes it one step further from instlux by avoiding buring the ISO AND modifying the HD partitions, but that also means that the installation process needs to be modified (hence lupin).

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-13
> **LurkerLinux said:**
> I was using herd4. tried it with both v8 and v9.

And I tried changing the menu.lst as well.

In v9 add the "pause_for_debug" kernel parameter in menu.lst  and at the shell type "uname -r" to find out the version of the kernel used. Herd4 kernel must be 2.6.20-8-generic, Herd5 2.6.20-9-generic. Anything different will not work.

---

### Post by Meny on 2007-03-13
alright I did some research and found 2 interesting things that might work for vista:

1. [[COLOR="Blue"]_here_[/COLOR]]("http://technet2.microsoft.com/WindowsVista/en/library/85cd5efe-c349-427c-b035-c2719d4af7781033.mspx?mfr=true") microsoft say
"Can I disable BCD entirely when I am not booting to Windows Vista?

No. The boot manager for Windows Vista runs first to determine which operating system to start. Therefore, if you want to boot to the older operating system, you must set the default order to the older operating system in the BCD store. For more information, see [How to change the default operating system entry]("http://technet2.microsoft.com/WindowsVista/en/library/85cd5efe-c349-427c-b035-c2719d4af7781033.mspx#BKMK_default")."

2. and [_[COLOR="Blue"]here[/COLOR]_]("http://www.911cd.net/forums//lofiversion/index.php?t18031.html") spacesurfer say
"Therefore:

If we rename grldr to ntldr, and replace XP's ntldr with the pseudo-ntldr, then we can get a Grub menu. This, then opens the door for loading other OS's.

So, although you can't place Vista on Grub menu, you can place Grub on Vista's menu to load other OS's.

Now, we need someone with Vista and XP dual booting to test this."

so between the two it seems to me (not that I know much about it) that it could work, I'll give it a try and report.

### Post by dbgeezer on 2007-03-13
> **ago said:**
> Did you reset also menu.lst as explained above?

Yes, if you are referring back to post 1270.

Grub stops on the first line, the 'find' command, because it can't find the linux file that is there.  It defaults to the first subdirectory that it finds in the wubi directory, which is disks.

....later....

I finally figured this out.  If you are getting an Error 17 and you have previously installed wubi, uninstall it, clean out your Recycle bin and reinstall it.  This will get past the Error 17.

There are other problems with the most recent versions.  Version 9 doesn't do it for me.  Version 8 works except the preseed.cfg is hosed.

I'm currently using v8 with a preseed.cfg from v9. 

... now back to the job I was working on.  I don't have ntfs access to my windows partition, which I could get if I had a network.

I don't have wireless access.  Is anybody using a Dell Inspiron E1505?  It looks like everything is installed properly, but the wireless comes up in a 'disabled' mode.  The function key that switches it on is a Windows based creature and I haven't found an Ubuntu method for turning it on.  Any clues?

Thanks!

### Post by nekra on 2007-03-14
Finally I was able to install with version 4/9  after resolving some problems:
- I have 4 partitions (XP, Vista 64, Vista 32 and BackUp) and as Vista is messing up the drive
 letters Wubi had a problem to find grldr on the same partition as boot.ini, so I copied it to all 4
 After install I got the confirmation that boot.ini is on the backup-drive (?), as this is the one I
 can see in Ubuntu,
-Wubi 4.9 did not create the disk-images, I went back to 4.7 and after 4.9 install, copied the
 saved images
-Wubi 4.6 to 4.9 don't extract initrd
Running Ubuntu, I have two problems: no way to activate my wireless connection and after
shutdown, Ubuntu is stopped, but the computer not.
But once You can overcome the alfa-problems and get a real working beta, this could be the
BIG breaktrough for Linux in the world of Windows Users.
Thank You

### Post by ago on 2007-03-14

> My installation will get to the blue screen but not any further. Attached are my log files and my preseed.cfg file. Can anyone tell me what is going wrong? I am using Wubi-7.04-herd4-v9.exe.

It does require Feisty Herd 5 ALTERNATE ISO ( [http://cdimage.ubuntu.com/releases/feisty/herd-5/feisty-alternate-i386.iso](http://cdimage.ubuntu.com/releases/feisty/herd-5/feisty-alternate-i386.iso) ) . ### Post by ago on 2007-03-14
> **Aliarse said:**
> Was just going to post about not being able to login, any solution to this yet? (one that hopefully doesnt involve a re-install :P)
reinstalling takes 10 minutes. You have to edit preseed.cfg before rebooting comment the crypted password and uncomment 

d-i passwd/user-password password ubuntu
d-i passwd/user-password-again password ubuntu


> Upon trying to restart however, i did get an error that stopped the restarting, dont know if this is a WBI problem, or herd5.
Looking into that it right now.

> Overall, i think this one is the best WBI yet, install went smooooooth, unlike previous versions i've tried! Good Work, keep it up. :)
Most of the changes were done by ecology thumbs up to his work. Still a few issues to address, but as mentioned, things should improve quite quickly now. We are getting there...

### Post by ago on 2007-03-14
to fix the reboot issue copy and paste the following command in a terminal

```
sudo /bin/sed -i 's/killall5/ps axwww \| grep -v ntfs-3g \| grep -v PID \| grep -v rc \| grep -v init \| awk "\{print \\\$1\}" \|  xargs -n1 kill/g' /etc/init.d/sendsigs
```

---

### Post by ago on 2007-03-15

yes that can probably be done, see the grub4dos docs for how to put it in MBR there is a wingrub utility to do that, but you run it at your own risk, we will probably wait for people upstream to make grldr compatible with Vista without having to touch the MBR. Vista is not an important target at the moment, nor will it be for quite a few months. Most people out ther use XP/W2K and that is where we focus. Of course if someone can come with a way to make grldr work on Vista we will add that as soon as viable.

### Post by ago on 2007-03-15
> **ago said:**
> Looks fine for what I can see, it is formatting the loopfile. It might be slow, but it seems ok. Do you have a sata/scsi disk?

### Post by ago on 2007-03-15
We need to know what is the driver required by your hard disk. The easiest way is probably to boot from Ubuntu LiveCD and send us the output of the following commands:

lspci -v >> log
lsmod >> log
dmesg >> log

Run the above and send us the log file which is generated.

---

### Post by HampusW on 2007-03-15
> **HampusW said:**
> Right now I'm planning to use libcurl for the actual downloading. It has alot of features, but is also small and fast. You can find more details about libcurl on their website: [http://curl.haxx.se/libcurl/]("http://curl.haxx.se/libcurl/"). libcurl supports both http and ftp downloads in a fast and simple way. It doesn't support metalinks or checksums on its own though, so my plugin will have to provide that functionality. I will use some kind of hash lib for the checksums and a xml library for the metalink parsing. Haven't decided about those yet. All in all I'm quite sure the plugin will take less than 1 MB and probable more like half a MB.

So far I've downloaded libcurl and compiled a static library out of it and I've made a test app with some basic libcurl functionality. Works excellent! I've also tested to compile the NSISdl plugin and that works fine too, so I know everything I need is working (except xml & hashes, which should be no problem). Now I just need to put it all together and fill in the missing details... It's just a matter of time until it's all done and working! I'll keep you updated on my progress.

I have made some progress now. My plugin is working and looks just like NSISdl (both for users and NSIS programmers). It uses libcurl for the downloading (as planned) and can download a file from an url now. My plugin can download from all protocols supported by libcurl (NSISdl can just download from HTTP).

It is far from done though. I am going to add support for metalinks (using expat for xml parsing) and checksums (using hashing code from XySSL). So far my plugin takes 186 kB (i.e. without any xml and hashing libs linked in). The finished plugin will probably take between 200 kB and 400 kB (depends on how much size expat will add to the dll).

If everything continues this way I will be done with the plugin within a few weeks. If you would like me to consider any additional features, feel free to ask! If I don't answer on the forum, you can always send me an email (hw@vox.nu).

---

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-16
> **Aquashark said:**
> installed with minefield3 and the partition Ubuntu is installed on (E:\ or sda5) isn't mounted on boot..

it can't be mounted by editing /etc/fstab, you can only mount it with sudo ntfs-3g etc.
but how do i unmount with ntfs-3g? because i'm getting errors at reboot if a partition is mounted  with ntfs-3g

1st get sure you have ntfs-3g installed. (it is not by default)
sudo apt-get install ntfs-3g

if you want to mount manually
sudo mkdir /media/mydrive
sudo ntfs-3g /dev/sda5 /media/mydrive

to unmount 
sudo umount /media/mydrive

---

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-16
> **Damo1976 said:**
> Hi,
How do I remove it?
Damo1976
Launch the installer once again. It will ask if you want to uninstall.
Check c:\, d:\ , e:\ and look if you have a wubi folder.
You can safely delete it.

### Post by Spegelius on 2007-03-18
Hello, my first post in these forums :). Great work you've done guys, it got me trying linux again. For me, partitioning hasn't been a problem technically, as i've been fiddling with computers ~10 years. It has, on the other hand, been a problem because i'm lazy ;) (byproduct of being a software engineer, after a day of coding, system maintenenace is not so thrilling anymore). Just to try linux has been too much work, but now it's easy. An it seems Linux is getting more userfriendly (and Dosbox keeps oldies at my fingers).

Installed few weeks back to my Compaq N800c laptop which has modified Win2003 Server (Wubi-herd4-v4 i think) and works great. Had some minor probs (errors during shutdown), but found help from this thread. Now trying to do a distribution upgrade, lets see how it goes. As i understood, i have to manually update kernel and initrd to wubi folder?

> **ComputersXP said:**
> Vista aint got a boot.ini file .. will only work onXP

Yep, the Vista bootmanager is wholly different. I've tried to get this working on my main machine (Opteron 175, 2GB, 150 Raptor) after i installed Vista. No luck there.
Installer does work ok as i had it running on this machine before Vista. But after Vista, GRUB just gives me errors about unsupported bootsector and can't find grldr... tried the trick mentioned before (rename grldr to ntldr), but just gives the 0xc000007b error. Partitions on my first hd:
Primary NTFS: Win2k3
Logical NTFS: Vista
Logical NTFS: rest of space

Funny thing about Vista bootmenu is that it automatically pics the Ubuntu-item from boot.ini (allthough it doesn't work).

Oh well, at least i can play with Ubuntu on my laptop. I am available if Vista solutions need testing.

### Post by ecology2007 on 2007-03-18
> As i understood, i have to manually update kernel and initrd to wubi folder?

This is a tricky thing, you don't want to do that.
Now, if you do want to do that, and if you don't know how, you have better wait until we come with an installer that allows uppgrades.

Just use the normal auto updates in the control center, and you will have allways have a top notch up to date system. A working initrd does need any upgrade.

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-18
Ago, I see that you are using uname -r to determine the kernel version to build the initrd using the lupin initrd builder script . Exactly how can I change this so that the outputted initrd version is different from the currently running kernel? This is necessary for allowing user-side kernel upgrades, which are necessary for installing the nvidia and ati drivers, dist-updates, usplash updates, kernel security updates, and many other tasks. A recursive find-replace of $(uname -r) to 2.6.20-12-generic on the lupin/scripts/build.sh script still hasn't fixed the issue; still outputting an initrd with the modules from 2.6.20-9-generic. Do you know of which files I must adjust to allow for generating an initrd for a different kernel version? Optimally, this should all be transparently done by mkinitramfs when installing a new kernel; the initrd should be transparently patched with the lupin patches, and the kernel, initrd and menu.lst should be automatically transferred over to the windows drive, so that the kernel/initrd can be updated from within ubuntu like it is in the standard ubuntu, instead of having to mess around with adjusting the files in windows, which is what I'm trying to get working.

---

### Post by tuxcantfly on 2007-03-18
> Where do I get the 2.6.20.11 kernel upgrade and how do I accomplish the upgrade within minefield4? 

Doesn't exist; you can't get it. I'm trying to replace the current hack for kernel upgrades with a proper one that updates the kernel like ubuntu does, only that'll require a couple of patches to our initrd building system. For now, wubi users will simply have to do without nvidia/ati drivers, usplash updates, kernel updates, dist-updates, and other things that require the regeneration of the initrd.

### Post by Spegelius on 2007-03-19
Building your own initrd and kernel seems to work, if you have access to 'real' Feisty installation (like VMware installation). I just updated to 2.6.20-12 version, by first building lupin in real Feisty and copying the files to Wubi folder. I also had to update the Wubi-installation with 2.6.20-12 related kernel stuff (headers, modules etc.) so Wubi finds them. The result, as i understood, is like a 'real' Feisty  updated installation (ok, the 'real' sounds ugle, but i mean a Feisty installed by normal means ;) ).
But i think it's a lot of hassle just to get a minor upgrade. And this is just a out-of-the-box kernel upgrade. If i'm not badly mistaken, building something kernel dependant (like drivers etc. mentioned before) would make this utterly hard, i guess there would be a lot of copying of modules etc. between Wubi-installation and the 're... dang, normal Feisty. But i'm no linux guru, so i might be wrong...
